questions.question: India adopted a policy of Import Substitution Industrialisation (ISI) after independence. Which of the following was a common limitation faced by ISI-led strategy by the 1980s?
What is the main goal of the PM Gati Shakti initiative?

In which year was 'Make in India' launched by the Government of India to facilitate investment, foster innovation, enhance skill development, protect intellectual property, and build best in class manufacturing infrastructure in India?
Which of the following is the new criteria for becoming a small enterprise in the definition of the Ministry of Micro, Small & Medium Enterprises (MSMEs) that was announced in Union Budget 2025-26?
Which industry is closely associated with 'cluster development' and the second-largest sector in terms of job creation in India after agriculture?
Which of the following categories of MSMEs constitutes the major share of MSME registration on the Udyam Registration Portal?
Which of the following options is a final good in the context of Indian economy?
Post 1991 economic reforms, India engaged more actively in global trade agreements. Which organisation did India join in 1995 to boost global trade integration?
In which year did the National Development Council (NDC) recommend a national policy on co-operatives?
After independence, co-operatives became an integral part of which planning initiative in India?
India's telecom revolution is a major outcome of liberalisation. Which regulatory authority was established in 1997 to oversee the telecom sector?

Which of the following is/are correct regarding the agricultural sector post the reform period of 1991?1. Quantitative restrictions on imports of agricultural products to India were fully removed from April 2001.2. There was a huge increase in the import duties on agricultural products to India after 1991.3. Due to the export-oriented policy after 1991, there was a shift from production for the domestic market towards production for the export market, with more focus on cash crops than food grains.
According to the State Bank of India (SBI) report on India’s poverty rate for 2024, what is the estimated national poverty rate?
As per the SDG India Index 2023-24, what was the estimated percentage of reduction in the population living in multidimensional poverty between 2015-16 and 2019-21?
According to the World Bank 2021 data, what was the poverty rate at $2.15 a day (2017 PPP) for India?
Which of the following correctly differentiates between absolute poverty and relative poverty?
questions.option_1: Absolute poverty is subjective; relative poverty is objective.
questions.option_2: There is no difference between the two.
questions.option_3: Absolute poverty is based on income inequality; relative poverty is not.
questions.option_4: Absolute poverty uses a fixed threshold; relative poverty compares individuals within a society.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the ten indicators used in calculating the Multidimensional Poverty Index (MPI)?
Which of the following measures the percentage of a country's population living below the poverty line?
Which statistical indicator is used to measure the depth of poverty by accounting for the extent to which individuals fall below the poverty line?
Which state had the highest multidimensional poverty index according to the National Multidimensional Poverty Index 2023?
According to the Economic Survey 2023-24, how many Indians are estimated to have escaped multidimensional poverty between 2015-16 and 2019-21?
According to NITI Aayog, what was the reduction in the Poverty Headcount Ratio in India from 2013-14 to 2022-23?
According to the NITI Aayog's discussion paper released in January 2024, which state recorded the highest number of people escaping multidimensional poverty between 2013-14 and 2022-23?
Disinvestment proceeds contribute to non-tax revenue of the government. Which body manages disinvestment in India?
Disinvestment was adopted as a tool for privatisation in India. Which of the following options best defines disinvestment?
questions.option_2: Selling government stakes in public sector enterprises
questions.option_3: Sale of public assets to foreign governments
questions.option_4: Purchase of private companies by the government
